title: oxalis albicans subsp. californica
intro: Oxalis albicans subsp. californica, also known as California wood-sorrel, is a perennial plant species native to California and Baja California. It belongs to the Oxalidaceae family and is characterized by its white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, to treat a range of health issues such as digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Oxalis albicans subsp. californica are often used to make teas, salves, and infusions that are believed to promote health benefits such as wound healing, anti-aging, and immune system support.
